summary: Prevent push-to-talk input from queuing a new recording while the recorder is arming, recording, transcribing, checking, or warming.

review_items.summary: Push-to-talk press events are accepted only from start states. Presses during arming, recording, transcription processing, status checking, or model warming are ignored and do not latch a pending recording.

While local Whisper or cloud Deepgram transcription is running, repeated push-to-talk clicks, key presses, or native shortcut events cannot start or queue another capture; entering transcription also clears any held-key state so a fresh press is required after the widget returns to a start state.
No database schema, migration, SQL policy, hosted API, auth, billing, or cross-project contract change is required.

patches.summary: Gate push-to-talk press handling so busy recorder states cannot set the held-key flag or queue a follow-up recording, and clear the held-key flag when transcription starts.

compatibility.notes: The change is local to the desktop recorder widget. Existing push-to-talk shortcuts, cancellation, local/cloud provider selection, and transcript insertion remain available.

data_handling.sends_audio_to_remote_service: unchanged from the selected transcription provider behavior
data_handling.stores_transcripts: The latest transcript continues to use local WebView result storage.
